cards.title: Managing Parental Anxiety on Family Holidays
cards.body: Anxiety about worst-case scenarios can overshadow family vacations, but real emergencies reveal our capacity to cope. Learning to balance worry with relaxation helps parents model healthy stress management for children.

cards.title: Breaking Cycles of Dysfunctional Parenting Patterns
cards.body: Children raised by narcissistic or avoidant parents often struggle to recognize healthy relationships in adulthood. Addressing these childhood patterns takes time and intentional work to develop secure attachment skills.

cards.title: Only Children and Family Prejudices Debunked
cards.body: Only children are often stereotyped as selfish, lonely, and maladjusted—prejudices that extend to their parents. Growing research shows only children develop normally and thrive without siblings.

cards.title: Affording Quality Education Without Expensive Tutoring
cards.body: Parental support and encouragement matter more than private tutoring for children's academic success. Parents should focus on their child's individual needs rather than projecting their own school experiences.

cards.title: Understanding Kids' Attention-Seeking Behavior
cards.body: Children's demands for attention aren't solved by stricter boundaries—they signal unmet emotional needs. Understanding the root cause of attention-seeking helps parents respond more effectively.

cards.title: Raising Decision-Makers in a Changing World
cards.body: Children's brains don't fully develop until age 25, so they need guidance making important choices. Parents can help kids develop good decision-making skills by understanding how their developing brains work.

cards.title: Supporting Neurodivergent Children Effectively
cards.body: Motivational interviewing respects children's autonomy while working toward their goals—ideal for neurodivergent kids. This approach emphasizes building on strengths rather than focusing on deficits.

cards.title: Healing From Verbal and Emotional Abuse by Parents
cards.body: Adult children experiencing parental verbal abuse often doubt themselves and feel guilty or anxious after interactions. Reclaiming your voice means setting boundaries and recognizing unhealthy patterns.

cards.title: School Rights During Extreme Heat Events
cards.body: Parents and workers have certain legal rights during dangerous heatwaves, including the option to keep children home. Understanding these rights helps families stay safe during extreme temperatures.

cards.title: Forced Adoption Accountability and Family Trauma
cards.body: Forced adoptions in Britain during the 1960s-70s caused lasting trauma to families and babies. Government apologies must be accompanied by accountability from all institutions involved.
